parts.text:
Hey, I’m glad we finally found a minute to catch up. I’ve been watching what you’re doing from the sidelines, and honestly, your progress is impressive—but because I care about you, I’m going to give you the "older sibling" talk before you hit a wall you didn't see coming.

Here’s the reality of where you are in your career right now: you have the talent, but you need to start thinking more about your **strategic positioning** than just your daily output.

First, stop saying "yes" to every project that comes across your desk. I know you want to be a team player, and I know you’re capable, but if you’re always the one doing the heavy lifting on the "maintenance" tasks, you’re never going to have the bandwidth for the high-visibility work that actually leads to a promotion. You need to start auditing your time. If a task doesn’t align with where you want to be in two years, try to delegate it or automate it.

Second—and this is something I had to learn the hard way—your work doesn’t speak for itself as loudly as you think it does. You have to be your own advocate. Start keeping a "win log." Every time you solve a major problem or hit a milestone, write it down with the specific metrics attached. When it comes time for your performance review, or when a new opportunity opens up, you won’t be scrambling to remember why you’re the best fit. You’ll have the receipts.

Third, look at your network. Not just the people at your level who you grab coffee with, but the people two steps ahead of you. Don't just ask them for "mentorship"—that's too vague. Ask them for specific feedback on a project or how they navigated a certain transition. People love to share their expertise, and those relationships are what get your name mentioned in rooms you aren’t in yet.

I’m telling you this because I’ve seen people with half your drive get further just because they played the game a bit smarter. You’ve got the engine; you just need to make sure you're steering in the right direction. 

Go take a walk, get some sunlight, and then sit down and look at your calendar for next week. If it’s 100% execution and 0% strategy, we need to fix that. 

I’m proud of you, but let’s get you to that next level. You’ve earned it.
